title: Nursing Research and Practice Development Working Group
content:
Nursing Research and Practice Development Working Group

Research Focus

Research Focus

It focuses on determining research priorities in nursing and conducting studies in line with these priorities. For a better nursing care; it aims to conduct research, develop tools and scales that evaluate nursing care outcomes. For the purpose of ensuring professional development; it supports the monitoring of conceptual models, defining education that supports nursing practice and determining strategies to increase research. For the purpose of better health for the community; works towards priorities including health promotion, women's health, child health, elderly population, chronic disease, terminal patients and their relatives, quality of life of caregivers. It aims to ensure that the researches conducted have scientific value, originality and the potential for new discoveries. It aims for research to contribute to human life, appeal to international understanding and gain national prestige.

This group is a group that aims to bring together faculty and staff of the Department of Nursing to make new contributions to the literature. In this group, it is aimed to plan research and projects to improve nursing care, to establish research groups, to carry out collaborative studies, to use different research methods, to report and publish research, and to carry out the necessary trainings.
